text: I used t-mobile for over ten years, switching to AT&T, t-mobile sucks now!! Even if you suspend the line you still have to pay $100/mo..

Literally the worst phone service ever. I haven’t had service this bad since dial up. Wait, dial up was better. How are you going to merge with a company better than yours and your worse than before you merged? The worst mistake of my life was switching from Verizon to T-Mobile and I have made some serious mistakes in my 39 years of life. Maybe fix your service before you merge with another service and fire all their employees. Pathetic. Hopefully this review goes through but it probably won’t because my service sucks!!

T-Mobile is a scam... they do nothing to help. Customer service gives you the run around and make you pay for xtra moths after you have cancelled their service!!! 
What ever you do , do not go with this con artist company!!!! They are the worst I have ever experienced!!! 
Awful
Absolute disgusting what they do to their customers!!!!
SOS 
Do not go to T-Mobile....
I can’t stand this company!!!!
If you don’t heed my warning and countless others, you will pay dearly out of your pocket!!!
Avoid this company at all costs!!!!
I hate T-Mobile...

I've only been with T-Mobile for only a short time and it is by far the worst service I've ever had. I can't make any phone calls in my house and I live in the city. My calls are constantly dropping!  Also, I spent an hour in a T-Mobile store trying to get an upgrade on my phone after already paying the early upgrade fee online and was told the system was down and to come back tomorrow. The next day I called to confirm that the system was back up and someone hung up on me. I decided to go to another store and spent another hour only to have the cs rep tell me that I needed to pay more money in order to upgrade. I decided to pay the remaining balance on the phone and upgraded to a new phone. After dealing with almost no coverage in my house and my phone not working properly, I decided to switch to another carrier. My phone has been working great ever since and I can actually talk on the phone EVERYWHERE... I called T-Mobile THREE times in a row to cancel my service. Each time I waited 30 minutes before being disconnected every single time... This is very bad business.

On Thursday, August 27th after texting for 2 days I was made aware by friends and family my text were not being received. I contacted T-Mobile regarding my present LG K10 and its cracked screen. I paid the $10 insurance fee and received a new LG K30 Friday, August 28th. I then traveled to the T-Mobile in Balch Springs to have it activated and a content transfer. After leaving the store I discover the K30 is also not sending my text messages. I call t-mobile support NUMEROUS times to find a resolution. None is forth coming. Finally, an LG representative  suggest I return the phone. Saturday morning August 29th I return to T-Mobile to purchase a Samsung Galaxy believing the LGs are malfunctioning. After paying $300 and increasing my monthly bill by $30 I get home to discover I can receive text but no one receives my text messages. I call T-Mobile suppprt again to  finally be told my phone number was submitted as one sending spam text. I profusely refuted this claim and asked that they check my text messages to confirm I had done no such thing. I was then informed it would be 10 days that I would be without texting capabities while they investigate. So now I've purchased a phone, protective  cover and car charger that I DON'T need! 

T-Mobile jeopardized communications with my job and family by not contacting me with the spam allegations. I did not receive a call, text or email concerning the matter. Only after I spent $300 on a new phone and increased my monthly payments ans calling support services numerous times was I given a reason for my phone's inability to respond to text messages. After 7 years of patronage  this is how I am treated. I deserve not only an apology but a refund for the purchase of the phone and accessories.

We started out with Sprint and T-Mobile bought them out for the last month our service has been going downhill they said to be patient it will get better I can’t believe they are running commercials saying it’s gonna be a better network how? I read up on who owns T-Mobile the Germans in the Japanese on 70% The other percentage who knows?

I've been a T-Mobile customer for many years and recently canceled because service in the area where I vacation is terrible.  My husband stayed with T-Mobile and is standing on a chair inside our camper attempting to get service. Service as horrible in northern Michigan. Customer reps are not very helpful either. I'm glad I left.

T-Mobile is the absolute bottom of the cell phone carriers. Avoid. We have paid them THOUSANDS for no service and for new apple products to not work. We are locked in and can’t get out. CS reps just apologize constantly- but NEVER HELP. Id love to one day not be apologized to by someone in India (definitely where their “customer service” routes the calls - you probably will barely understand their “English” and that’s IF you call on a land line - just give up ever hearing on T-Mobile cell phones)...

If you could give 0 stars I would have!  I left T-mobile because of their terrible customer service and billing practices.   Went to Sprint ( I loved them, never had a problem) then they became T-mobile and immediately I am having billing problems again. 
Do not get trapped with T-mobile you will REGRET it!

Terrible service, they will charge you on service for items you don’t have. They don’t recommend the best options for you to save. They cheated me out of a years worth service when I didn’t have the item.

I have been a T Mobile customer for years and still enjoy the service but I have noticed that when I make a call or someone who is a t-mobile user, a greeting says, “thank you for using T-mobile prepaid.” Everyone doesn’t need to know my carrier or plan. It’s offensive and prejudice and I would prefer that greeting to be taken off.

We had T mobile for a few years. At first we had problems with service so the rep GAVE us a booster. A few years later we had to change carriers because we moved and T mobile didn't provide service where we moved to. Then we received a bill for $316 for the "FREE " booster that was several years old by then. I called and they would not budge a bit and was pretty rude. They turned it over to collections the day after we received the bill.... Then the next day they took it from our bank account ( without our permission) . Be very careful using T mobile. They will charge you for so called " free" items if you change carriers.

So Richard from T-Mobile at valley fair mall was very helpful! He help me and my mom figure out which kind of tablet to get and was multitasking helping out another lady that has came in before and he was quick about it.

I moved to T-Mobile from Verzion about 1.5 years ago. I was on a budget and really like the idea of no hidden taxes and fees. My bill is $102.00/month for four lines (military). The value seemed unbeatable when I switched; free Netflix is a huge plus as well. There's a catch though. 

T-Mobile's network, comparative to others (like Verizon for example), is absolute garbage. They might claim to be the "fastest growing" and a "leader in 5G" and what have you, but I can assure you that none of those words actually matter in real-world, day-to-day use. 

In my experience, any large city is a safe bet for T-Mobile (and any other carrier, really). But your mileage varies GREATLY as soon as you get outside of big population centers. It was immediately obvious to me that my service was extremely degraded after switching. Areas where I was comfortably receiving LTE with Verizon before were now sitting in "Extended" or "No Service". My T-Mobile plan is barely usable in my house and requires the use of WiFi calling to make any normal conversation.

Lastly, T-Mobile's service goes down more than any other carrier I've had. The reason I write this review now is because I have had zero service in the town I live in for the last 3 days. I can only use my phone via WiFi right now. This has happened a few times this year, and more than ten times since I started with them 1.5 years ago.

While the value might seem great, I'd rather spend the extra money on a plan I know I can rely on. $100/mo for four lines sounds fantastic, until you get those four lines and none of them work.

I wish there were a zero. Worst phone company I ever have. If I dont have internet at home, i cant make or receive calls or text mesg. And in the end, plans are not cheaper. Get Verizon. Cant wait  to dump their ass once my phones are paid off.

Since I have to give them a star. This company has been the worst company. I got a phone and tablet on July 24th and dropped my phone and submitted my claim July 28th and it has been a challenge since then. I received a text today saying my phone bill is $212.19 (say what). I could go on and on about the service but I will get angry all over again but  just let me say this t-mobile, "say what you mean and mean what you say".

I have been a customer of Sprint for many years with no issues with connection speed (LTE), drop calls, etc. Ever since the merger with T-mobile I haven't been able to get LTE service and randomly my phone goes into Emergency calls only mode and drops network connection all together.  For the amount I am paying I am not getting the service I am paying for.  That is called bait and switch.   I'll be contacting a lawyer.
Awful worst.  Automated system is terrible on-line experience is awful.  If sprint was available i would switch over.  VERIZON HERE I COME!

We have been trying to work with TMobile to move away from this terrible carrier for over 6 hours.  The first 3 agents had no idea on how to do anything.  Of course we had to wait hours to even get anyone.  I have used TMobile since they started.  They merged with Sprint and are now the worst customer service in the industry.

I wish I could give zero stars. I've been a happy customer with Sprint for years. Of course, as soon as T-Mobile takes over their customer service and policies are the ABSOLUTE WORST. I needed to upgrade one phone on my account. With T-Mobile's upgrade policy, it was a terrible deal, despite being done with my lease on my current device. Rather than pay the crazy fees T-Mobile was asking, I asked them if I could cancel my whole account without penalty, even though I was mid-lease on another device. They said yes that's fine you can cancel without penalty. I repeated, "Even though I'm in the middle of my lease on this phone, I can just turn it in and walk away?" They said "Yup, you can." I tried to call customer service to confirm because that seemed too easy to get away from T-Mobile. Waited on hold a total of 3 calls and 45 minutes, each time getting disconnected with no explanation. Just hold music and then call ended. I tried to online chat and was given, "Due to high volume, we can't accept your chat request. Please try again later." So I went ahead and switched to another provider and ported my number. I go in to return the phone and am told I need to pay $486 to end my lease and turn in the phone, or $1000 and own the phone. The same guy was there who told me I could turn it in without penalty, who of course "Doesn't remember that conversation at all." I have no patience for lazy, careless, and ignorant customer service employees, having worked in the industry for 15 years myself. Don't run your mouth and tell a customer something unless you double check the system and confirm you are giving them accurate information. I am out $500 because some employee was too lazy to bother actually checking my account and confirming and because T-Mobile can't actually answer their phone or chat to provide any level of customer support. So disappointing on both counts and I will continue to avoid doing business with T-Mobile at all costs forever in the future. Shame on you!

Man called I told him what phone's we have. He was only to happy to take my money. Then after hours of being on the phone at one time 4 hrs. And day's of him not having service. His flip phone wasn't compatible. I went through buyer remorse it's been over a month and several more phone call's and still no money sent back. I hate to be lied to, if you can't make your customers happy then don't lie to them.

Had an issue with my phone and Tommy, located at the Plano store was kind and very helpful.   He fixed the issue and explained how I can fix it should it happen again.  Thank you Tommy.  You provided wonderful customer service!

They are committing fraud with their claim of 3G. I pay for a hotspot and since the merger have barely gotten dial up speed. Disgusting. Will be looking for a new provider. Tech told us hotspot not for streaming???? Really, that's not what you told us when you took our money! Tech support admits the speed is Not 3G.

Warning people, this scam cell phone company wasted my time . It’s not fit for wealthy or serious business. They taking poor people. Run and ruin there credit then turn them down . They have sucks credit department managed by kids unprofessional stay away never apply t - trash 
Also they using At&t (the worst company in North America antenna so reception very bad ) 
Don’t waste you time !

People beware!!
They are unprofessional came from Europe and shame by using at&t trash company anthena ans devices  they have no technical support or reception and cheating on bills ! 
Also never give them your social number !! 
Stay away !!

text: Since the merge T-Mobile customer service is the worst. The employees are lazy, slow talking and class-less it's as if they are high and can barely respond in a quick efficient manner. Horrible. Just horrible. They need to hire new educated professional people to answer the phones. After giving up and hanging up on a few we eventually found someone who had some class and education.

Horrible.  Sprint switched to T mobile and it's a nightmare. I payed 1000 and was told we don't qualify for an upgrade on our phones and I canceled a line and my bill is now higher.  Can't believe this.

Before the merger I had been with Sprint since the 90's.  I love Sprint, had NO problems.  Since technology allow us to watch tv on the phones, no problem.  Since the merger, my phone keep disconnecting from internet, call won't connect and when I'm searching the internet, its searching.  I read on here that people were with T-mobile and they felt like i did before the merger.  But after the merger we all feel the same.  I understand no service is trouble-free but this is crazy.  I have 4 lines as soon as my contract is up, im gone.

I have been a T-Mobile customer for 15 years. I have always had excellent reception in our area in Santa Monica. Since the merger with Sprint I am lucky to be able to get any reception in our apartment. I subscribe to the international plan so I can call my parents overseas. I used to have a perfect connection but now I consistently get disconnected. To say I am disappointed an understatement. I am not sure what I am paying for any more.

Thanks to Nadia and Maria at T-mobile, Jefferson Davis hwy! These girls spent a lot of time making things great for my son and myself! They are both rock stars and are definitely on their game! We will just stop in to say Hi, because these girls made a great impression on both of us! Above and beyond! You have our service for life and we will probably be switching our cable as well! Thanx again!

tmobile 6 years ago was for me one of the best, today it is the worst company that exists there is no use being their customer for so many years and always having my account up to date I never pay late and I have JOD and it is the worst What I did I have the maximum credit for them and it is useless I wanted to change my iphone pro max for the new note20 ultra and they ask me for 549 dollars of down payment is something absurd I still have the perfect credit, I went to att and they offer it to me without pay a penny that is to be a good company, I have 5 lines in my account and it is useless, bye tmobile for sure
I have spent three days dealing with this bull honky and after standing in the store another two hours last night as soon as I left the store everything they've done was automatically undone.

I had an inquiry about SIM card replacement. Truly expected an automated response, did get a person though. It was via chat, 7:02pm NY time, anyhow, she was so pleasant, gave me the info. I asked if I could do it in store versus online so I can have my SIM card replaced by someone who actually knows what they are doing. She let me know the total would go up by $10- which is reasonable and if we were still good? What?! She actually did a mental check in with me?! I know our interaction was brief, but after all day on the phone for work myself with a high target, fast paced demand, this little check in was so personable and very welcoming. Liaa, you managed in just seconds to deliver awesome service. Did her job, but added that human touch. Never know who may be on the other side of the screen (as in us customers), and she delivered.

I cannot begin to tell you how many problems I've had throughout the years but I was a loyal loyal customer of theirs I stuck with them over 8 years I had my number transferred to that company long story short after eight long years they decided to cut me off because of roaming as I'm traveling to and from different state to protect my family that's in Pennsylvania not only that I'm traveling to New York during these times of needs of the covid-19 without telling me anyting extremely upset about it. When I called in the past about issues with the same issue that they shut me off with they were telling me once I go to and from New York it will reset the phone and not to worry about this connection. Now they tell me they cannot give me my number back or connect my line again due to the roaming when I was never told about it very disgraceful to T-Mobile I have never ever been upset and dislike a company so much let alone that you guys did not contact me or train your staff to tell the truth. When I was on the phone with them numerous times disgraceful disgraceful!!!!

I went into the Tmobile store located at 370 E. Gunilla Rd. in the Bronx, NY. I purchased a new phone and the experience was less than pleasant. The associate I dealt with needed a lot of help completing the transaction and had to ask for help from another associate who seemed to barely know what to do themselves. During the transaction my associate takes another customer and begins to help them in the middle of helping me. After that another employee (who I believe was a manager) was at the counter when another customer came in with an issue and they told the associate that was supposed to be dealing with me to also try to resolve the other customers issue as well. Absolutely very little to no customer service at all. If I owned this store I would do an undercover boss scene. I would send my friends and family in to see things. It's nothing to them to give you a terrible experience. If you're in the Bronx, I would probably find a different store.

Rude store reps! Store reps do not know what they are selling! I am literally paying for a phone I traded in in mint condition because rep failed to tell me that I had an installment not a lease! Crazy whack contract to steal from consumers!

Left Verizon a little over two months ago and went with T-Mobile worst mistake I've ever made this is by far the worst phone service I've ever had half the time I cannot get data connection and I have unlimited data probably end up going back with Verizon or anybody other than T-Mobile

I signed up for their service a while back, and their customer service and network is horrible. I purchased a phone from them and the mobile network was unreliable. I tried to return the phone and it took 3 months to get a refund! It was no fault of mine. There was a lot of false promises, and one hand not knowing what the other hand was doing. It was a headache and a mess with this company as they are too unorganized. If I didn't keep records of all my transactions and conversations, I would not have gotten my money back. Avoid T-Mobile because they are incompetent and their service is horrible.
Horrible experience everyone in there was very rude had no concern about any issues on my phone! Will be switching carriers! Exeter store is where this occurred with CJ

BAD really bad T-mobile service sucks, it really sucks they are taking over sprint. My grandma 80 years old had sprint was able to use her sprint phone at her house she went to upgrade her phone get a new line took her T-mobile phone didn’t work at her house, she tired to take the phone back and they gave her $30. Back out of $225. She paid. So wrong they ripped off a senior!!! Do not recommend at all.  I also have sprint and my calls have been dropping recently a lot. I have been a sprint customer for 23 years. If this continues I’m going somewhere else since sprint is turning into team mobile and I’m telling everyone I know about my experience. I went with my grandma and I can’t believed this happened. She should have got all her money back. She was ripped off!!!!!!!!!! T-mobile is a bad company taking money from old ladies.

Terrible service! We had business account with Verizon and were pitched by the T-Mobile sales person to switch over. He said he would pay off existing equipment payment agreements with Verizon, and that the T-Mobile cellular service is very good. None of this happened after the switch. Reception and quality of service was the worst we had ever experienced. Once day they even had a full day outage in the T-Mobie network. We lost tons of business that day. Then it turned out they would not pay off anything with Verizon but gave us credits on T-Mobile bill. I feel it was bite and switch technique. Very deceptive. I will never sign with T-Mobile again. Currently we went back to Verizon. Be ware!

T-Mobile is any thing but quick thinking .  Their customer service uses alot of "double talk" and the customer service reps like to Blind transfer you so you end up repeating yourself to several reps.
Very bad experience with T-Mobile they still charge 10 dollars each month even though they automatically take money out of your account for monthly charge very awful company

TMOBILE IS THE SHADIEST PHONE 
" PROVIDER " OUT THERE WHO DON'T VALUE THEIR CUSTOMERS BUSINESS AND THEIR DESPICABLE, RUTHLESS, PRICE GOUGING WITH HIDDEN CHARGES THAT THEY TRY SNEAKING IN FOR NO ACTUAL REASON FOR THE CHARGES, TRYING, AND SUCCEEDING IN RIPPING THEIR CUSTOMERS OFF DURING A WORLDWIDE GLOBAL DEADLY HEALTH PANDEMIC AND TAKING ADVANTAGE OF PEOPLE TRYING TO GET BY , BY USING DISGUSTING BUSINESS PRACTICES LIKE TAKING A PAYMENT OUT OF SOMEONE'S ACCOUNT BEFORE IT WAS EVEN DUE AND THEN SHUT OFF SERVICE AFTER 11PM AT THE WORST POSSIBLE TIME FOR ME BECAUSE THEY'RE CLOSED ( CONVENIENTLY) AND I COULDN'T DO ANYTHING ABOUT IT AND THEN I REALIZED THAT I COULD SEE VIA THEIR WEBSITE AND HOPEFULLY FIND A CHAT THAT'S AVAILABLE 24HRS FOR CUSTOMERS AND OF COURSE THERE ISN'T BUT HERE'S WHAT I SAW THAT ABSOLUTELY APPALLED ME AND THEY WERE DEMANDING THAT I MAKE A PAYMENT ON TOP OF THE ONE THEY JUST RECIEVED FROM ME OUT OF MY AUTO PAY  JUST A FEW HOURS EARLIER!! I'M BEYOND LIVID THAT THEY ARE TRYING TO ESSENTIALLY STEAL MONEY THAT WAS TO PAY MY BILL AND THEN DEMAND THE SAME IDENTICAL CHARGE FOR ME TO PAY AGAIN FOR NOTHING AND NOW I'M STUCK WITH NO PHONE SERVICE EVEN THOUGH I PAID FOR IT..UNTIL THEY OPEN AND THEN HAVE TO ACTUALLY HAVE TO DEAL WITH THESE CROOKS WHEN ALL I WANT TO DO IS SOMETHING THAT WON'T BE USEFUL ALTHOUGH I'D FEEL BETTER IF THAT WERE AN OPTION! TMOBILE TREATS THEIR CUSTOMERS NO MATTER IF YOU'RE A LONG TIME LOYALTY CUSTOMER OR A RECENT ONE THE SAME...LIKE DOLLAR SIGNS, OPPORTUNITIES TO RIP OFF AND GARBAGE!!
** UPDATED REVIEW MUST READ
 I JUST TRIED TO GET A LIVE REPRESENTATIVE VIA THE CHAT LINE ON TMOBILE WEBSITE AND I WAS EAGERLY GREETED QUICKLY AND SOO FAKE WITH HER SICKENINGLY SWEET ATTITUDE AND ASKED HOW COULD SHE MAKE MY DAY GREAT? OH WOW, YEAH SO ONCE I START TELLING HER HOW TMOBILE TOOK MONEY FROM ME UNAUTHORIZED AND THEN SHUT OFF MY SERVICE..SHE *( HER AGENT REPRESENTATIVE NAME IS APPARENTLY 
" QUEEN ") AS IN QUEEN OF TELLING ME A BUNCH OFBS...SO AS SOON AS I LET HER KNOW THAT NO QUEEN I'M NOT GOING TO BUY A PHONE FROM YOU SO YOU CAN STOP RIGHT NOW AND JUST GET CUSTOMER SERVICE..SHE TRIED TO BLOW ME OFF BY PROMISING TO GET ME TO A CUSTOMER SERVICE REPRESENTATIVE AND TO JUST LOG IN AND I SAID NO BECAUSE YOU'RE GOING TO DISCONNECT ME AS SOON AS I DO...FINALLY AFTER SWEARING UP AND DOWN SHE WASN'T GOING TO DO THAT...YEP STUPID ME BELIEVING HER BECAUSE AS SOON AS I LOGGED IN SHE HUNG UP ON ME RIGHT IN MY FACE!!! I HOPE THAT QUEEN OF DISRESPECTFUL TRASH GETS FIRED! I HATE YOU'RE COMPANY MORE THAN I THOUGHT POSSIBLE UNTIL I SPOKE AND GOT HUNG UP ON AFTER TRYING TO GET HELP!!!!

I had a problem with it after 3 weeks. The tmobile store They could not do anything for me. I went to the store and they called the 1800 number for me to talk to customer service. Then they decided to replace the brand new phone with a refurbished phone. I had a 50 dollar screen protection. The manager could not give me the screen protection replacement. Called customer service same responce. If you  get your phone from Sam's club you will save tons of money. If you go to Costco you will save tons of money. This profection plan between 15 and 20 dollars a month. You get the same plan from Costco for 80 dollars one time payment for the lifetime of the pbone. equals 500 dollars from tmobile for 2 years. If your phone go bad costco return policy is 1 month. If you go to Walmart you buy all accessories half the price and you get the same equal service tomobile store provide so why pay extra. T-Mobile do not honor customer loyalty. tmobile is a horrible company i can not wait to go to another company. 
I wish I can give them zero star

RUN don’t walk to another provider!! We have been with sprint for 15 years. When they merged with T-Mobile my husbands iPhone 8 started dropping calls, couldn’t get texts in or out etc. 3 trips to sprint stores, 1 to T-Mobile store & 7 calls to customer service we still have no solution. All we get is squeaky voices saying “no worries”! And “let me connect you to our technical team”, who promised to send a new sims card but never did. We have been without this phone for a month & the best we’ve gotten is a measly 20.00 credit to our account?! Do yourself a favor & go anywhere but here. ✌️Out sprint!

I was searching for a new provider and reached to T-Mobile. They required all kinds of things to set up an account. I was told I would get a refund if the service did not work. Learned from a friend that the service would not work in my area and so I reached back to cancel it before I even got started. Took forever to cancel, but finally they did refund my deposit. A month later I got an email that they had successfully ported my number. What? And I have to start over with my current provider and have been without a phone for five days. They did nothing to help. Avoid this company at all costs.

On hold to get through to customer service for a hour and 39 minutes.  On the phone speaking to agent that clearly is in another country because he sounds like he's 8970000 miles away and his accent is so thick I had to ask him several times to repeat himself while I was explaining that T-Mobile lost all of my voice messages on three phones, two of which are business phones and after needing on the phone for 39 minutes call disconnected.  He calls back but thre phone never rings so I get an alert I have a message gettin an 800 # it's fun T-Mobile. Cam you image!! Over two hours down the toilet and do you think thre foreigner calls me back again because the val CLEARLY WENT STRAIGHT TO VOICEMAIL BUT NOOOOO I hate you T-Mobile I hope they pass a law to not employ other countries to work for US companies overseas because  Not only does it suck I can't understand them but we have people in our country that need jobs and I don't like that they can access  My personal information in different countries are is probly someone with my name driving a dam 2020 Honda Accord in another country under my name!!!
I pay for data and I can’t even use it. I keep complaining to T-Mobile and the “fix” the speed issues and still I can’t even load a simple website. Worst phone company

Ever since T-Mobile paired with sprint I don’t get service. I left sprint because of their awful service and here I am again. T-Mobile used to be the best and I loved it. It’s too bad they sided with the devil. I will be getting rid of T-Mobile as soon as I graduate!!! Horrible idea and horrible service. I don’t even get the service in my room in the middle of town! I have to stay on my WiFi

Worst company you can possible work with!

They told lies when they try to sell you their plan, couldn't deliver the promise after you ordered, and then the customer support call afterwards is basically "unfortunately we can do nothing". The supervisor was extremely rude and even mocked me on the phone that I was about to cry. Steer clear from tmobile and save your self some hassles.

IM FED UP WITH T MOBILE. BEEN WITH THEM FOR 5 YEARS AND FOR THE PAST 3 WEEKS IM ONLY GETTING 1 BAR. CALLED CUSTOMER SERVICE AND MY ISSUE HASNT BEEN RESOLVED. THIS IS NOT THE FIRST TIME THIS HAS HAPPENED. IM DEFINITELY THINKING ABOUT SWITCHING OVER TO ANOTHER CARRIER. BYE T MOBILE ✌🏾

Tmobile the new Sprint; a lying unethical deceptive company .They engage in bait and switch practices,and illegally accessed and   checked my credit when I forbid them to do so when I  requested them to ONLY transfer my service to a backup device. Tmobile is being sued by New York state and more class actions to follow. Avoid TMobile it is a lying unethical duplicitous company that will fail like Sprint and Hertz.
I hate this company been with em for 13 years and it amazes me every year they get worse and  put out crap products at expensive prices . Hoping to move on from em soon as my my phone is paid off

The customer service os HORRIBLE you will wait HOURS on the phone for any help.  When I finally got a tech to help he dropped my call and never called me back as he said he would.  I'm going back to Verizon.

T-mobile worse service. Nothing but dropped calls on the daily for over 2 year. Would have switched sooner if he could have paid off the phones. Now they are charging us for service we don't even use. The customer service is the most ghetto service I have received.

Horrible! I am a legacy Sprint customer. Since T-Mobile took over, I've had numerous dropped calls and very poor reception. My flex lease is not being honored by them and they are requiring me to pay half down to upgrade a phone. Customer service is equally horrible. Rather than solve a problem they just read a script and repeat the same thing over and over. That is, if you can get them on the phone.

This service and company is the very worst! I have been a valued long term Sprint customer and this merger has been a terrible experience.. My phone has not worked correctly in the past week, I have called, chatted, and even went to a location today.. and nothing works! I am at a loss and will be moving to AT&T by next week!!!

I was a sprint customer and agreed to switch being as though they were gonna merge anyway so they were doing a promo at the rodeo in Houston and the young man Kevin assured me my bill would be lower and all I'd have to do is turn in all my phones they'd pay off all except mine a total $500 it should've been and instead I have 1500 dollar sprint bill smh as soon as i pay off these phone I'm going to at&t! The way they handle everything is deceitful and I haven't been able to talk to anyone after I gave them a good review 2 days after I got my phone

I repeat do not use T-Mobile my 84 year old mom went and paid her phone they gladly accepted her money I forgot to tell her her plan is no longer available and refuse to refund her money unless you wants to buy a new phone and a whole new plan nice customer service T-Mobile sucks

First off, I got with T-mobile and was with them for awhile and I started off with T-mobile was I was in KY so I was moving to TN and I was also trying to upgrade to a new phone and asked the gentleman that was helping me if i was going to get service where I was going because I was going to a small town and the lady that wasn't helping me but was standing right there said yeah yeah you'll get service so i believed her and went with the phone. Then I came down to TN for a short trip before moving and I had absolutely had NO service anywhere down here so when I got back to KY I went back to the store to return the phone, I was pissed because the lady at the location lied to me. I wouldn't have upgraded if I knew I wasn't going to have service. So I told another location about what happened and he said wait to see what other service is better for down there before returning the phone so I hated that I had NO service down here at all anywhere. That other location was great, they was helpful. So I moved to TN and I decided to go with Verizon so when I called customer service to cancel they said I didn't have a contract and couldn't help me about canceling the phone and told me a tower was being built, HAHA sorry but there was NO tower being built within 24-48 hours when my son was in school and I had to have some type of signal to be able to talk to his teacher if needed if something happened. The supervisor got on the phone and was talking about the tower this and that. She was rude and lied to me and was NOT helpful at all. So I told her I was canceling my service and I'm turning the phone back in regardless because there was NO tower being built when I needed signal for my son being in school. Was NOT happy with her customer service, especially being a supervisor. She shouldn't be in that role. So I went with a different company and the next day I had to drive 45 mins back to T-mobile with that being the closest location for me to return the phone because the manager wasn't in that day so I came back and the manager at that location in TN was very helpful and returned it for me, the phone was in great condition so he took it back. I receive a bill from you guys which was fine, was my last bill. So I come back out again to pay it and I asked the guy that was working so once I pay this I'm 100% done with this account right, he looked at it and said yes you are 100% paid off. So I left. Later on I sold the phone that I still had from T-mobile because I needed the money for bills and I was already told that it was 100% paid off. Then I had to talk with T-mobile to unlock it and went through whole process and wait and this and that and the lady I talked to asked if it was paid off and said yes I went to a location and its paid off and she says yes I see that its paid off so she did the process to unlock it. So left it alone. About a few weeks or so later I get ANOTHER bill about how I still owe on the phone!! WHAT THE HELL!! So I call T-mobile and ask why and she kept repeating herself that I still owe this and that. WHAT!!?? I told her, I went to the location and the guy told me it was paid off and the lady I spoke with that was in the process of unlocking said was paid off as well. SO all of you are LIARS! When I tried to tell the lady from customer service all that happened since the beginning she said there was NO notes in there. SO y'all delete them or don't write them down because 2 people told me it was paid off and all of a sudden i owe money?? With all this happening after it happened, COVID happened. I have had NO job since moving to this small town and everything was closed for awhile and y'all still telling me i owe y'all $381.00 when i was TOLD from 2 people that it was paid off and I owe nothing else!! HORRIBLE customer service!!! I was happy with  T-mobile till moving to TN, then everything after that has been crap ! With having NO job and COVID happened, y'all put my so say bill into collections! That is complete BS! You all have lied to me and BS me and not even doing your job right and BAD customer service. I will NOT recommend you all and as I see on your reviews people are NOT happy with you all anyways. I WILL keep leaving bad reviews until this is taken care of off collections! I can see why are not happy because you lie, don't write down notes like you should and bad customer service. For the bad customer service, lying and not doing your job and I was told by 2 people it was 100% paid off. I shouldn't have to go through this, It should be taken as a loss on your side because all this is complete BS. If I could rate this company a 0 star I would because after all this that I had to go through, you all don't deserve a rating!

I went online and chatted with an agent and tried to figure out how much 4 lines was going to cost me. After 35 minutes of going around in circles She said she was unable to give me an exact number. So I asked who would be able to give me that information. She gave me a phone number to call I called they were unable to give me that information. So I asked for a supervisor. I was then routed to three more people that were not supervisors when all's I was asking for a work as supervisor and then I was hung up on. That was a 45-minute call . I called back finally got what I was told was a supervisor and I don't believe it at this point. And she said she would escalate a ticket. And when I tried to get the exact pricing from her she said I would have to switch to them to get the exact price. Why in the world would I switch when I don't know how much I'm going to pay. I told her after this customer service I would gladly pay my current carrier more money because at least they give me good customer service. I have been in sales almost my whole life and never when somebody ask for the bottom dollar have I not been able to give them the bottom dollar that is not how you do business and I am so glad I found this out before I started giving this company my money. Because if they're going to treat me that way when I'm trying to give them money how are they going to treat me when I'm already giving them my money.
The  worst customer service, I am going to move to different company. 0 for customer service.

HORRIBLE!I have been with Tmobile many many years and have 5 lines. For some reason I am unable to use my phone or internet. I can only use internet when using home wifi. When me or anyone else leave home we cant use internet from our phone. Has anyone ever had this problem with At&t Verizon? I try to call and of course no answer. I'm thinking about switching. My job depends on it.

Dear T-Mobile client service . 

I don’t know if I should say « dear » , when I’ve been treated so badly by your company these past years and especially this past month. I am usually polite but considering the fact that one of your worker (Salomé) hang up the phone to my father while he was talking ,
I am now quite annoyed. (That’s the least I can say) . To explain the problem, I signed for a contract that mentioned that my 4g was unlimited , went to France to see my family (where there is no internet) , and was working from home due to the coronavirus situation. I did the exact same thing last year , and never got any issues , my 4g was unlimited , but this year « somehow » I only got 18GB , which is a ridiculous amount considering that I am paying over 80€ per month to get unlimited data. The worst thing is that , on my t mobile account it is written that I could use my bundles  data . I was then told that « the rules changed » and that there wasn’t any unlimited 4g anymore when traveling. So first of all, you can be sure I’ll let anybody know how t-mobile is cooperating with the coronavirus situation , and trust me that I do know a lot of people that will love to hear about it. Second , because I didn’t have any internet anymore , I called Sncf (which is the train company un France ) to get a ticket back to the Netherlands ,(  I had no other choice than to call them since I didn’t have any internet anymore ) . Sncf is a free service in France , but your company charged me 3€ per minute !! I called 22 minutes and got a bill of 65 euros !! Who do you think you are ?! I could have reached an alien on mars for that price ! 
I am telling you that I will leave your company as soon as I can and never come back ! You are just a big scam , who are enjoying the fact that the entire world is having a sanitary crises to fill up your pockets ! I ll leave attached a picture of the bill and you’ll see that even on it it it written « unlimited data and bellen »., unlimited if I pay 1,92€ per day ?? It s not unlimited !  LIARS . I hope your company will drown as much as you try to drown people .

I thought our love were mutual @tmobile but now looks like it's very painful and service is not improving but declining rapidly. Not to have anyone monkey around with APN and other wireless settings and ip and dns. Text messages are not going thru and now service calls on the road are choppy. On the other hand VERIZON has the same offering and I am going to divorce you and look for another provider.
Horrible service and customer service was rude and didnt do a damn thing to help will never go back and would not tell anybody to go with them

T Mobile bought Sprint and now I have sh!t for service. I hope the CEO and its investors get COVID-19.

Tmobile is SATAN. The service has gone from great to okay to better to crap over the last 12 yrs. The customer service has always been crap but at least the phone service was okay. SInce taking over sprint the service here in Virginia had gone to hell in a hand basket. It shutdowns for days or hours and they can't say why or at least give us a credit for the lost time. 

I travel a LOT. I lose my signal 3 or 4 times just between RIchmond Va and Va Beach Va on I64. That is so unexceptable because there are always accidents and breakdowns on 64. If I need to call AAA or my family I can't get a signal. If I go into Marshall's or Walmart I can't get a signal and I don't have a flip phone I have an Iphone 11. 

Recently I lost my deceased mother's phone line due to ignorant employees. I have kept her phone alive to listen to her voicemail etc. I was told they could add 5th phone line for free and I agreed. ONLY to find out that it was costiing me 30.00 a month. 

Now here is the deal, I added my daughter by porting her # to my mom's line because the idiot didnt say hey miss lady we're having a special and you can add a new line at no cost to you. SHE just kept saying i can make your mom's line go away and replace it with your daughter's number. I said okay even though i wasn't really prepaired to let mom go. so what i got was a 5th line with a new number and the girl deleted mom's line and ported my daugher's in moms slot.

now i have this new line with a new # for no reason. why didn't they just use common sense and put my daughter's # on the 5th line and leave mom's alone is the frustating million dollar question.

it then took almost a week to port my daughter's number over to tmobile.  they said she could have insurance but it never was added. so then we had to do a thrid rodeo do-see-do dance to get that fixed. and the girl robin or tiffany said oh your 5th line is free and you shouldn't cancel it because we're having a promotion . i agreed to keep it for my new ipad purchase.

now i've already cried tears on the phone with robin or tiffany because it was traumatic letting go of my mom. i know it was just a voicemail but it kept her alive for me. don't judge just walk a mile in my shoes. this is the 3rd/4th person i've had this conversation with my emotions are running high. 

And she promised me everything was fine. i asked are you sure because EVERY time you talk to a tmobile rep in person or over the phone you get railroaded with lies. Then when they don't do what they say they will and you have to call back the next person has NEVER EVER heard of such a thing as the person before has told you!!!!  

Now TODAY i get my bill and its gone up 50.00!!! 30.00 for the new "free" line and 20.00 for the insurance on the iphone. so i call to fix it. I am so done and all my emotions come back of all this trauma every time i call tmobile. Had i not been fighting back tears of frustration and dispair. I would have just said cancel this 5th line since it is causing so much evil in my life. But i was focused on the 50.00 increase. and the rep never suggested it either. SHe took me for granted and i feel as though each rep is only out for #1 which means how can they get a bonius or something out of each phone call and customer.  

So TODAY this other dumb untrained never heard of customer service before individual railroads me into changing my plan. oh you get the same service, oh nothing will change, oh you won't lose your personal hotspot. oh its 5.00 cheaper and 25.00 cheaper with autopay. That's 5 lines for 145.00 a month. 

AFter she changes my plan she let's me know the full details and i see red!! i can't get my old grandfathered plan back. I'm not happy with this new crap where i am being downgraded and slowed down and expected to pay 25/30.00 extra a month for what i got for free with my old plan!!! I freaking hate tmobile and their non trained employees. 

oh i forgot to mention when i went into the store there was a tmobile employee yelling and cussing at a customer. it was a young latino guy employed by tmobile screaming at an elderly african american man. he was yelling, "oh i know you you always come in here with issues and you never pay your bill on time etc.etc." NOW here's the kicker. I couldn't get a signal inside the tmobile store to facetime or snapchat their arguement the signal just circled around and around. 

Stay away from tmobile!!! Now that they killed my mother a second time i am going to shop around for better service. You should too!!!!!

Usiness or put it up on Google or calendar or Wakefield express and have a list of at risk customer services tell loc Al health authority so they can send a group email to GPS if I can get my act together as someone mentally ill and cognitive issues I'm sure you can I did years ago do a customer service level course and passed this is not good customer service please don't let it happen again thanks for making me miss my gp and being ill  this evening my health is a big issue at moment if I want to ring samaritans I need that mobile to work so I can go outside to use phone as I can't use my home phone at 3am in case neighbours downstairs complain I'm in sheltered housing not being able to ring could cost me my life

I dont have much complaints for the the 15 year I ve been with them 2002- 2015 and 2018 till now, but I have noticed twice so far that they literally make up false charges on your account, especially when you are signed up for a automatic payment withdrawal every month. And if you dont carefully watch what s going on, you will miss and they will keep on stealing from the millions of clients they have. this month i was $30, next month could be $3 but it still a theft.   This month I was charged  $138 instead of the $109 I am supposed to pay. I immediately noticed and called to see what s going on and I was informed that they did some changes on my account (unauthorized of course, and without my knowledge) and this is why my bill was $30 extra. They apologized and issued a credit,. this is the second time I caught them cheating by making up false charges and they always apologize and refund them. But the only reason i noticed was because both times was amounts over $20. If it was something like $5-$10, I d never noticed. This is a SCAM, a theft, Be careful with them! Always watch your bills. If I catch them once again, I am done for good with them.

I went to T-Mobil store at river park sugar land texas  and the two hispanic employees "help" me to get service and promotions. For them to get my business they lie they omit info. concern the promotion. And the conversation was in spanish it sound to good to be true so I ask them to explain to my son (he is 35 years old) in english they said  the same. first/second bill was ok  but later tmobil said the promotion that they offered me was expired at the time that they offered.

I bought a Galaxy S20 Ultra from T-Mobile on 6/19/2020. The representative told me that I would be able to have insurance on my phone with a prepaid account. I went through the whole transaction and then come to find out a few days later when I reviewed my account that I did not have insurance. I called and the representative and they told me that prepaid accounts do not have an insurance option available. 

So yesterday, 8/1/2020, I woke up to tiny cracks appearing across my screen. I went to the T-Mobile store once again and they said that they wanted to charge me $40 for them to even look at the phone. I am so confused as to how a company of this size cannot offer the simplest of conveniences such as insurance on a phone. I also do not understand how they could not have been more clear in the beginning of our interactions that I needed to find out how to get approved for another type of account. I would have taken that option and paid whatever deposits needed to be paid. Now my phone is not a “new line” so would not get the supposed perks of a new account that I would have had in the beginning. 

I should have gone to another carrier, even the most basic carriers today have insurance and better customer service. I now have to live day to day wondering if something happens to my phone I will be out almost $2000 in an instant. Who has that type of money running around? If you need or want insurance on a phone and but still want the convenience of prepaid, DO NOT choose T-Mobile. They are definitely the “un-carrier” if you ask me, they do not care at all.
